description: Letter from Yisrael b. Natan (Isrā'īl b. Sahlūn), in Jerusalem, to Nahray b. Nissim, in Fustat. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: 31 December 1061 CE, according to Gil. Yisrael mentions letters intended for the Maghrib that he had previously sent, in particular one for a certain Labrāṭ. He asks Nahray to send him the garment that arrived from Tripoli and the medicines (ʿaqāqīr) and the bitumen (qifār) with Abū ʿAlī Ismāʿīl b. Ruʿbūb. Umm Sha'ul, the wife of Nahray, is in Jerusalem, and Yisrael conveys news of her and other contacts. There is a famine in Jerusalem. Yisrael conveys the good news that Abūn b. Ṣadaqa and his daughter have recovered from illness—but Yisrael is sick of Abūn. ASE
tags: Jerusalem, mail-order medicine, illness: recovered, communal strife, illness letter 969-1517, Illness
description_he: מכתב מישראל בן נתן, ירושלים, לנהריי בן נסים, פוסטאט, 31 בדצמבר 1061. על מכתבים שנשלחו למגרב, בקשת בגד ותרופות. מדווח על רעב בירושלים ועל אשת נהריי שנמצאת שם.
